mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold crossing fails to predict direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion across durable and non-durable sectors, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production during an economic expansion regime reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during expansion regimes yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in > 50% of observations, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with an increasing federal funds rate creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and margin erosion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising inflation and rates fails to predict negative returns with > 60% accuracy, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals a strengthening labor market that supports aggregate demand and corporate hiring, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth crosses above 2.0 percent, it confirms robust economic expansion that sustains corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect is not statistically significant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and an increase in the unemployment rate indicates a broad economic contraction that reduces business investment and consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal in supply and labor markets fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.
